title: UserRecord
description: A Yomp user's Firestore document (collection `users`, doc id = uid). PARTIAL-PATCH model: every top-level field is OPTIONAL (no root `required[]`) because writes patch a subset. All stored values are PRIMITIVE — there are deliberately NO string enums in this schema, so out-of-domain values (e.g. a future provider or theme) deserialise as plain strings on both platforms and can never crash a typed enum. Source of truth for both yomp-next (TS) and yomp-android (Kotlin).

Timestamp.description: Opaque Firestore server Timestamp. The neutral schema leaves it untyped; build.mjs aliases it per platform because the Firestore SDK deserialises it into a platform-native Timestamp on read. TS narrows each timestamp to `Timestamp | FieldValue | null` (read | serverTimestamp() write sentinel | cleared) but keeps it OPTIONAL; Kotlin keeps nullable `com.google.firebase.Timestamp?` (no FieldValue data-class equivalent).

DogBirthday.description: Canonical birthday for the singular `dog`. month + year are required; day is optional (some users give only month/year).

Dog.description: Canonical single-dog shape (native + active web). birthday is canonical; dob is legacy.

DogListEntry.description: LEGACY web multi-dog list. Not written by native v1. Reconcile with singular `dog` in a future migration.

provider.description: Auth provider id. Should be an AUTH_PROVIDERS value (see account.json) but is a plain string — validated at the app layer, never a typed enum.

dogs.description: Legacy multi-dog list, originally web; also written by native when a user has 2+ dogs. Element shape `{id, name, breed, size}` is load-bearing: web `getDogs()` drops elements lacking a string id + name. Server caps the list at 10 elements.

preferredCategories.description: Each element should be a CATEGORY_KEYS value; consumers must filter unknown values on read.

themeMode.description: One of THEME_MODES. Writing any other value is rejected by Firestore rules.

acquisitionSource.description: Opaque acquisition/attribution payload — shape intentionally unconstrained.

usernameDisplay.description: Display-cased form of `username` (e.g. "TomWalks") for presentation; the canonical `username` stays lowercase (join key + uniqueness doc id). OPTIONAL — legacy lowercase users have none; the claim API writes it on a new claim. Allows UPPERCASE, unlike the canonical handle. The pattern documents the Firestore-rules constraint (`^[a-zA-Z0-9_]{3,20}$`); the build is type-only so it is not propagated into the generated types.

role.description: Whether this account is a standard user or a business claiming venues. Plain string — validated at the app layer via ROLES constants; never a typed enum.
